calendar.matters:
Motion For A Protective Order To Reconvene The Preservation Deposition Of Robert F. Lyman That Commenced On November 8, 2007
Off Calendar Dec-18-2007 Advanced From Jan-03-08 To Dec-27-07 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 612

Motion For A Protective Order To Reconvene The Preservation Deposition Of Robert F. Lyman That Commenced On November 8, 2007
Off Calendar Dec-27-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
John Goldstein, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Samantha Lyman
Molly J. Mrowka, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Montello, Inc.
Catherine Morris Krow, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Union Carbide Corp.

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted and modified: Petitioner Samantha Lyman's Motion For A Protective Order To Conclude The Preservation Deposition Of Robert F. Lyman Or, in the alternative, To Reconvene The Deposition And Limit The Scope Of The Deposition is granted as it seeks to reconvene the deposition.
As to all questions regarding what Plaintiff's counsel told Plaintiff about Montello products at any time prior to and in anticipation of Plaintiff's declaration, any privilege has been waived by the declaration submitted in the summary judgment motion. Defendants also properly may propound questions regarding the length of meetings with Plaintiff prior to his deposition.
Plaintiff shall bear all costs of the reconvened deposition. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Amended Petition Of Samantha Lyman To Perpetuate Testimony Of Robert F. Lyman By Deposition.
Off Calendar Oct-18-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
James P. Nevin, Esq., appeared for Petitioner & moving party: Samantha Lyman
Natasha Cupp, Esq., appeared for Respondent & opposing party: Union Carbide
Molly J, Mrowka, Esq., appeared for Respondent & opposing party: Montello, Inc.
Mary Kirk Hillyard, Esq., appeared for Respondent & opposing party: Drilling Specialties Co., LLC.

Ruling:
Hearing held. Petition of Samantha Lyman to perpetuate testimony of Robert F. Lyman by deposition is GRANTED for the wrongful death action - the testimony shall not be admissible in any other proceeding as the court has previously denied requests for further deposition testimony in the personal injury case. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

payments.description: REQ OR STIP AND ORDER W/O HEARING
